concepts.name: AI Taxonomy
concepts.description: Classification of AI levels, from classic rule-based systems to agentic systems.

concepts.name: Self-Attention Mechanism
concepts.description: Core mechanism of Transformer computing similarity between tokens using Q, K, V matrices.

concepts.name: Transformer Architecture
concepts.description: Revolutionary deep learning architecture based on self-attention, covering encoder and decoder models.

concepts.name: Token Economy
concepts.description: Understanding tokenization, context window limits, and cost/latency trade-offs in LLM API usage.

concepts.name: AI Product Lifecycle
concepts.description: The end-to-end process of discovering, statements, building, and deploying human-centered AI products.

concepts.name: Rule/Workflow/Agent Selection
concepts.description: Decision matrix to choose between Rule-based, Workflow, or Agentic architectures based on task complexity.

concepts.name: ReAct Reasoning Pattern
concepts.description: Reasoning and acting loop (Thought-Action-Observation) for autonomous agents.

concepts.name: Tool Calling API
concepts.description: How LLMs declare, parse, and execute external tools using structured JSON schemas.

concepts.name: Prompt Engineering
concepts.description: Techniques for guiding LLMs, including system prompts, XML structuring, few-shot learning, and Chain-of-Thought.

concepts.name: Context Engineering
concepts.description: Strategies to manage context window, mitigate Lost-in-the-Middle phenomenon, and compress history.

concepts.name: AI Product Uncertainty & UX
concepts.description: Designing user experiences that handle LLM uncertainty, precision/recall trade-offs, and graceful failures.

concepts.name: Vector Embeddings
concepts.description: Mathematical representation of text in high-dimensional vector spaces, measured by cosine similarity.

concepts.name: Vector Databases
concepts.description: Specialized databases for indexing, storing, and performing fast similarity search on vector embeddings.

concepts.name: Basic RAG Pipeline
concepts.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation basics, including document ingestion, chunking, and context-based prompting.

concepts.name: Advanced RAG Pipelines
concepts.description: Advanced techniques such as query transformation, reranking, hybrid search, and semantic caching.

concepts.name: Multi-Agent Architectures
concepts.description: Designing systems with multiple cooperating agents, routers, and specialized coordinators.

concepts.name: Data Pipelines & Observability
concepts.description: Data pipelines for ETL operations, logging, and observability of LLM interactions.

concepts.name: AI Guardrails & Safety
concepts.description: Input/output validation, prompt injection detection, and content filtering for safe AI deployment.

concepts.name: Cloud Infrastructure for Agents
concepts.description: Deploying agents onto cloud platforms using serverless functions, Docker containers, and scalable backends.

concepts.name: LLM Observability Stack
concepts.description: Monitoring latency, token usage, cost tracking, and system performance at production scale.

concepts.name: AI Evaluation & Benchmarking
concepts.description: Constructing golden datasets, LLM-as-a-judge patterns, and quantitative evaluation of AI systems.

concepts.name: [T1] PRD & Product-Market Fit
concepts.description: Analyzing product requirements and identifying PMF for AI applications.

concepts.name: [T2] AI Data Ingestion Strategy
concepts.description: Designing robust ingestion pipelines and data models for unstructured and structured AI datasets.

concepts.name: [T3] Agent Memory Systems
concepts.description: Managing short-term state, conversational history, and long-term vector/relational memories for agents.

concepts.name: [T1] AI Financial Modeling & ROI
concepts.description: Calculating return on investment, compute cost projection, and token cost economics for AI features.

concepts.name: [T2] Data Lakehouses & Production Data
concepts.description: Implementing data lakehouses (e.g. Delta Lake, Iceberg) and production pipeline design for massive scale AI apps.

concepts.name: [T3] Production RAG Pipelines
concepts.description: Building reliable retrieval architectures with advanced document parsing and chunking for production environments.

concepts.name: [T1] Stakeholder Management & Communication
concepts.description: Aligning internal teams, managing user expectations, and presenting AI model trade-offs to stakeholders.

concepts.name: [T2] Vector & Feature Stores
concepts.description: Using feature stores and vector databases to manage low-latency data access and embeddings for AI models.

concepts.name: [T3] GraphRAG & Knowledge Graphs
concepts.description: Combining Knowledge Graphs with RAG, using entity/relation extraction and graph traversals for deeper queries.

concepts.name: [T1] Roadmap Execution
concepts.description: Structuring milestones, handling uncertainties, and executing agile iterations for AI systems.

concepts.name: [T2] Model Serving & Optimization
concepts.description: Inference optimization techniques including quantization, batching, and high-throughput engines like vLLM.

concepts.name: [T3] Multi-Agent Architectures
concepts.description: Designing systems with multiple cooperating agents, routers, and specialized coordinators.

concepts.name: [T1] AI Governance & Risk
concepts.description: Assessing algorithmic bias, intellectual property, data lineage, and risk mitigation strategies in AI products.

concepts.name: [T2] CI/CD for AI Systems
concepts.description: Continuous integration and deployment pipelines for ML models, data schemas, and backend applications.

concepts.name: [T3] LoRA & QLoRA Fine-tuning
concepts.description: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods for customizing LLMs with low computational cost.

concepts.name: [T1] AI Compliance
concepts.description: Ensuring products adhere to global standards (e.g. EU AI Act, GDPR, local regulations) and compliance audits.

concepts.name: [T2] LLMOps & Prompt Versioning
concepts.description: Continuous integration, deployment, and versioning of prompts, schemas, and models in production.

concepts.name: [T3] LLM Alignment & DPO
concepts.description: Direct Preference Optimization and human alignment strategies to steer model behavior.

concepts.name: [T1] AI Adoption Tactics
concepts.description: Designing change management strategies and educational materials to increase user adoption of AI systems.

concepts.name: [T2] LLM Monitoring & Observability Stack
concepts.description: Monitoring latency, token usage, cost tracking, and system performance at production scale.

concepts.name: [T3] LangGraph Agents & Workflows
concepts.description: Implementing complex stateful multi-agent systems and cyclic workflows using LangGraph.

concepts.name: [T1] Responsible AI & Ethics
concepts.description: Applying ethical guidelines, mitigating biases, and ensuring transparency in user-facing AI applications.

concepts.name: [T2] Data Governance & Security
concepts.description: Applying data security controls, access policies, encryption, and classification of sensitive AI data.

concepts.name: [T3] Ragas & Guardrails Evaluation
concepts.description: Quantitative framework to evaluate RAG pipelines (faithfulness, relevancy) and runtime safety guardrails.

relations.evidence: Knowing the AI Taxonomy (Bot, Chatbot, Agent) is prerequisite to selecting the appropriate solution level for a task.
Self-attention is the core component of the Transformer architecture.
One must understand Self-attention mechanism before studying the entire Transformer block.
Self-attention is used for processing sequences inside Transformer encoder/decoder layers.
Transformer architectures define the sequence and output tokens, which dictates token economy constraints.
Token economy limitations and pricing models necessitate proper context engineering to minimize token usage.
Understanding the AI product lifecycle guides the choice of solution level (Rule vs Workflow vs Agent) during the design phase.
The AI Product Lifecycle framework is prerequisite to defining product requirements (PRD) and finding Product-Market Fit (PMF).
Identifying that a task requires an agentic level of autonomy is prerequisite to implementing the ReAct pattern.
ReAct agents require memory systems (short-term and long-term) to function over long horizons.
Tool calling is a prerequisite for the Action phase of the ReAct (Reasoning and Acting) loop.
Prompt engineering foundations lead into advanced context engineering techniques (e.g. Lost-in-the-Middle mitigation).
Prompt engineering is a prerequisite to design system instructions and prompt protection guardrails.
Proper context engineering helps mitigate model hallucination and uncertainty, which influences UX design.
Understanding vector embeddings is prerequisite to indexing and querying them in a vector database.
Vector embeddings are used for semantic search and retrieval in a basic RAG pipeline.
A vector database (like Chroma) is prerequisite to storing and retrieving chunks in a basic RAG architecture.
Basic RAG (chunking and vector search) is the foundation and prerequisite to advanced RAG (query transformation, reranking).
Basic RAG is compared with advanced RAG approaches in terms of retrieval quality and latency.
Basic RAG concepts are prerequisite to production-grade RAG pipeline optimizations.
Observability and ETL pipelines are prerequisite to constructing an advanced AI data ingestion strategy.
Establishing PRD and PMF is prerequisite to conducting financial modeling and ROI analysis for AI features.
Data ingestion strategies are prerequisite to building production-grade data lakehouses (e.g., Delta Lake, Iceberg).
Understanding agent memory systems is prerequisite to implementing production-ready RAG with persistent session memory.
Financial models and ROI projections are prerequisite to stakeholder alignment and expectation management.
Lakehouses store unstructured data that is processed into vector feature stores for real-time model queries.
Lakehouse data repositories store the raw data and extracted entities that are fused to construct GraphRAG knowledge graphs.
Standard production RAG pipelines are a prerequisite to building advanced GraphRAG using entity/relation knowledge graphs.
Aligning stakeholders is prerequisite to executing the AI roadmap and milestone tracking.
Feature stores provide optimized input features required for low-latency served models.
Vector stores manage low-latency embeddings needed by production RAG pipelines.
Knowledge graphs and GraphRAG serve as structured, queryable memory pools needed for complex multi-agent architectures.
Executing the roadmap must be governed by risk assessment and algorithmic bias reviews.
Model serving configurations and optimizations are versioned and deployed through automated CI/CD pipelines.
Customizing individual agent models using LoRA/QLoRA is prerequisite to optimizing the collective behavior of a multi-agent system.
Risk management is a prerequisite to meeting strict regulatory standards and compliance audits (e.g. EU AI Act).
CI/CD automation is prerequisite to standardizing LLMOps workflows, prompt versioning, and deployment registries.
Fine-tuning base models using parameter-efficient methods (LoRA/QLoRA) is prerequisite to preference alignment via DPO/ORPO.
Compliant AI systems are prerequisite to creating user trust and driving AI adoption within organizations.
LLMOps pipelines feed and register metrics monitored in LLM production monitoring environments.
Preference-aligned models are a prerequisite for robust performance in cyclic stateful multi-agent orchestrations like LangGraph.
Promoting AI adoption requires adhering to responsible and ethical AI development guidelines.
System logs and observability metrics are analyzed for compliance with data governance policies and security controls.
Stateful agentic workflows are evaluated quantitatively using the Ragas evaluation suite and protected by safety guardrails.
Ragas and guardrails evaluation tools enforce and verify Responsible AI metrics in real-time.
